A café in the neighbourhood

Coffee, checkerboard tiles, and a corner that's already yours.

vida and is the kind of place where the espresso machine runs before the doors properly open, the crayon jar comes out before the menu does, and somebody's always claimed the seat by the jazz.

Get Directions Call to Say Hi
The Room

Built for lingering, not for looking at your watch.

Walk past the red door and the checkerboard cabinetry, and you'll find hand-glazed ceramic ware doing the job regular crockery never could — every cup a little different, striped in red and blue like they were dipped by hand. Because they were.

An old popcorn tin holds the pens. A worn, leather-bound pad takes your order. Nothing here was bought to look curated — it just collected, the way a good room does when people keep coming back to it.

Slow shots, hand-glazed cups.

On the Table

What's usually in front of you

No two pours look quite alike — that's the point of a cup made by hand.

The House Pour

Latte, hand-poured

Steamed milk, a slow heart, and a shot pulled the way the regulars like it — in a cup that's never quite the same twice.

Over Ice

The layered iced latte

Cold brew and cream, stacked until it looks better than it should. It never lasts long enough to keep looking.

On the Side

Whatever's fresh that day

A short stack of cookies, still a little warm, next to whatever's steaming in front of you.

Ambience

Jazz on the speakers, the city through the glass

Visit

Come find the corner

Follow the checkerboard tiles in through the red door — we'll already have something on the machine.
